title: FYI â January 2020
body_text:
FYI â January 2020
FYI â January 2020
The following updates were made to the Payment Solutions Manual.
Access and Administration Volume
2-Step Security Part
Section 4.2, Registering a Soft Token
We added a note to explain that when a soft token is being replaced, users must delete the old token from their authenticator app and then install the new soft token.
User Management Part
Section 2.2,
Frequently Asked Questions
Question 5 â We clarified that there is a one to two minute delay when passwords are reset.
Section 8.5, Removing a 2-Step Security Token from a User
We added clarification that removal of a 2-step security token applies to any situation where a user no longer requires that token (e.g., user no longer needs access; user has a new cell phone).
If a token is being replaced, the existing token must first be deactivated, and then a new token assigned. For soft token replacements, the user will need to delete the old token from their authenticator app.
Once a new soft token has been assigned by a User Management Administrator, the user will be prompted to register their new soft token at the next login attempt. In the authenticator app, the user will choose to add another token and may need to select
Scan a Barcode
if not automatically prompted. As there is more than one authenticator app that can be used, the specific steps may differ slightly.
Document Library
Status
Payor's PAD Agreement
1696
We updated the instruction on obtaining valid AFT transaction codes. These are now located in CPA Standard 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files.
Revised
